snippets.title: Discriminative vs generative API shape (conceptual)
snippets.explanation: Same ML pipeline mindset (embed, model, deploy), but discriminative APIs return a constrained prediction; generative APIs return newly sampled sequences. Full LLM mechanics are covered under llms-fundamentals.

snippets.title: Sampling as the generative step
snippets.code:
import numpy as np

# Toy categorical distribution learned from counts
probs = np.array([0.5, 0.3, 0.2])  # next-token probabilities

# Generation = draw from the model
token_id = np.random.choice(len(probs), p=probs)
print("sampled token index:", token_id)
snippets.explanation: At inference, many generative models repeatedly choose the next piece of data from a predicted distribution. This is the operational difference from argmax classification.

snippets.title: When generation is not the product
snippets.explanation: A generative-capable architecture can be used discriminatively. Taxonomy follows the deployed capability—here, scoring, not synthesis.
